title: Multiple definition error after update to 5.014
body:
I have the following compiling error with 5.014:
```
/usr/bin/ld: CMakeFiles/secworks-aes.dir/CMakeFiles/secworks-aes.dir/aes_core.dir/aes_core___024root__DepSet_h4dea7a1b__1__Slow.cpp.o: in function `aes_core___024root___eval_static__TOP__0(aes_core___024roo
t*)':
aes_core___024root__DepSet_h4dea7a1b__1__Slow.cpp:(.text+0x25): multiple definition of `aes_core___024root___eval_static__TOP__0(aes_core___024root*)'; CMakeFiles/secworks-aes.dir/CMakeFiles/secworks-aes.di
r/aes_core.dir/aes_core___024root__DepSet_h4dea7a1b__0__Slow.cpp.o:aes_core___024root__DepSet_h4dea7a1b__0__Slow.cpp:(.text+0x8f80): first defined here
/usr/bin/ld: CMakeFiles/secworks-aes.dir/CMakeFiles/secworks-aes.dir/aes_core.dir/aes_core___024root__DepSet_h4dea7a1b__2__Slow.cpp.o: in function `aes_core___024root___eval_static__TOP__0(aes_core___024roo
t*)':
aes_core___024root__DepSet_h4dea7a1b__2__Slow.cpp:(.text+0x0): multiple definition of `aes_core___024root___eval_static__TOP__0(aes_core___024root*)'; CMakeFiles/secworks-aes.dir/CMakeFiles/secworks-aes.dir
/aes_core.dir/aes_core___024root__DepSet_h4dea7a1b__0__Slow.cpp.o:aes_core___024root__DepSet_h4dea7a1b__0__Slow.cpp:(.text+0x8f80): first defined here
/usr/bin/ld: CMakeFiles/secworks-aes.dir/CMakeFiles/secworks-aes.dir/aes_core.dir/aes_core___024root__DepSet_h4dea7a1b__3__Slow.cpp.o: in function `aes_core___024root___eval_static__TOP__0(aes_core___024roo
t*)':
aes_core___024root__DepSet_h4dea7a1b__3__Slow.cpp:(.text+0x0): multiple definition of `aes_core___024root___eval_static__TOP__0(aes_core___024root*)'; CMakeFiles/secworks-aes.dir/CMakeFiles/secworks-aes.dir
/aes_core.dir/aes_core___024root__DepSet_h4dea7a1b__0__Slow.cpp.o:aes_core___024root__DepSet_h4dea7a1b__0__Slow.cpp:(.text+0x8f80): first defined here
/usr/bin/ld: CMakeFiles/secworks-aes.dir/CMakeFiles/secworks-aes.dir/aes_core.dir/aes_core___024root__DepSet_h4dea7a1b__4__Slow.cpp.o: in function `aes_core___024root___eval_static__TOP__0(aes_core___024roo
t*)':
aes_core___024root__DepSet_h4dea7a1b__4__Slow.cpp:(.text+0x0): multiple definition of `aes_core___024root___eval_static__TOP__0(aes_core___024root*)'; CMakeFiles/secworks-aes.dir/CMakeFiles/secworks-aes.dir
/aes_core.dir/aes_core___024root__DepSet_h4dea7a1b__0__Slow.cpp.o:aes_core___024root__DepSet_h4dea7a1b__0__Slow.cpp:(.text+0x8f80): first defined here
```

And the same code compiles fine in 5.012.

Looks it caused by code like this:
```verilog
`ifdef ASSIGNS
    reg Z_f /* verilator public_flat_rw */ = 1'b0;
    reg Z_v /* verilator public_flat_rw */ = 1'b0;
    assign Z = Z_f ? Z_v : (A ^ B);
`else
    assign Z = (A ^ B);
`endif
```
It works without `ASSIGNS`, but causes the error above if its defined.

Here is the minimal reproducible example: [example.zip](https://github.com/verilator/verilator/files/12321501/example.zip)
The example using [freepdk](https://github.com/mflowgen/freepdk-45nm/blob/d3be42a72ca6eabff54a4f3be50c16613f69f1b6/stdcells.lib) library converted to verilog and netlist generated from https://github.com/secworks/aes.
It will report the mentioned error if compiled with `-DASSIGNS` passed to `VERILATOR_ARGS`. And compiles fine without the define.
